LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT BLUE RHINO GLOBAL SOURCING, INC. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WARNING THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Blue Rhino Global Sourcing, Inc.
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Blue Rhino Global Sourcing, Inc.

GRANTED. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant A&J Manufacturing, Llc
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant A&J Manufacturing, LLC.

GRANTED. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the Health & Safety Code.

Order is signed. Plaintiff is to submit a proposed judgment.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
